display.name: CloudNativePG
display.description: CloudNativePG cluster health from the native cnpg_* instance metrics: connections, throughput, cache, replication, WAL & archiving, checkpoints, storage, and the metrics collector itself.

display.name: pod
display.description: Filter by CloudNativePG instance pod.

display.name: datname
display.description: Filter per-database panels by database name (datname label).

display.name: Time Since Last Archive / Failure
display.description: Seconds since the last successful archival and since the last failure, per instance. The success age sawtooths as WAL is archived; a steadily climbing value means archiving stalled.

display.name: WAL Archive Status
display.description: WAL segments waiting to be archived (ready) vs. archived (done). A growing ready count means archiving is falling behind.

display.name: WAL Archiver Ops /s
display.description: Successful vs. failed WAL archive operations per second. Any sustained failure rate needs investigation.

display.name: Backends by Database
display.description: Backend connections grouped by database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: Backends by State
display.description: Backend connections grouped by state (active, idle, idle in transaction, …).

display.name: Backends Waiting on Locks
display.description: Backends blocked waiting on other queries. Sustained values point to lock contention.

display.name: Block I/O Time /s
display.description: Milliseconds per second spent reading and writing data blocks (requires track_io_timing). Rising read time points to disk pressure.

display.name: Block I/O /s
display.description: Buffer cache hits vs. disk block reads per second.

display.name: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio
display.description: Shared-buffer cache hit ratio from blks_hit / (blks_hit + blks_read) * 100. Below ~99% on an OLTP workload suggests undersized shared_buffers.

display.name: Buffers Written /s
display.description: Buffers written per second by the checkpointer (during checkpoints) and the background writer, plus buffers allocated.

display.name: Checkpoint Write / Sync Time /s
display.description: Milliseconds per second spent writing and syncing buffers during checkpoints.

display.name: Checkpoints /s
display.description: Timed (scheduled) vs. requested checkpoints per second. A high requested rate means checkpoints are being forced by WAL volume — consider raising max_wal_size.

display.name: Metrics Collection Duration
display.description: Time the CNPG exporter takes to collect metrics from PostgreSQL, by collector. Rising duration can indicate database pressure.

display.name: Metrics Collections /s
display.description: Rate of PostgreSQL metric collection cycles per instance.

display.name: Database Size
display.description: Disk space consumed per database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: Deadlocks & Conflicts /s
display.description: Deadlocks detected and queries canceled due to recovery conflicts (standby) per second. Any sustained value needs investigation.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''
pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: Longest Transaction by Database
display.description: Maximum transaction duration per database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: MultiXact ID Age
display.description: Multixact age from the frozen multixact ID per database. Like transaction-ID age, climbing values risk multixact wraparound; vacuum keeps it bounded.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: Replication Lag (standby)
display.description: Replica lag behind the primary in seconds, per standby instance.

display.name: Write / Flush / Replay Lag (primary view)
display.description: Per-replica write, flush, and replay lag in seconds as seen by the primary, grouped by replica application name.

display.name: Replication Slot Lag
display.description: WAL retained per replication slot in bytes (restart-LSN diff). Growing lag on an inactive slot risks unbounded WAL accumulation.

display.name: Replication Status
display.description: Per-instance recovery state (1 = standby) and WAL receiver up (1 = streaming) flags.

display.name: Temp File Bytes /s
display.description: Temporary file bytes written per second, by database. High values mean queries are spilling to disk (raise work_mem).

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: Temp Files /s
display.description: Temporary files created per second, by database.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: Tuple Activity /s
display.description: Rows returned, fetched, inserted, updated, and deleted per second.

display.name: Transactions /s
display.description: Committed and rolled-back transactions per second across selected databases.

display.name: WAL Segment Count
display.description: Number of WAL segments in pg_wal, per instance.

display.name: WAL Directory Size
display.description: Total size of the pg_wal directory in bytes, per instance.

display.name: Transaction ID Age
display.description: Transactions since the frozen XID per database. Climbing toward ~2.1 billion risks transaction-ID wraparound — vacuum should keep this well below.

filter.expression: pod IN $pod AND datname IN $datname AND datname != ''

display.name: WAL Archive Age
display.description: Seconds since the last successful WAL archival. A rising value means archiving has stalled.

display.name: Total Backends
display.description: Total backend connections across selected instances, all states. The Backends by State panel breaks this down.

display.name: Collection Errors
display.description: 1 if the last metrics collection on any instance ended with an error.

display.name: Total DB Size
display.description: Sum of all database sizes on the primary.

display.name: Fencing
display.description: 1 if any instance is fenced (isolated from the cluster for maintenance/recovery). Should normally be 0.

display.name: Longest Transaction
display.description: Duration of the longest-running transaction. Long transactions hold locks and block vacuum.

display.name: Nodes Used
display.description: Distinct Kubernetes nodes hosting the cluster's instances. A value of 1 with multiple instances means no high-availability node spread.

display.name: Replica Mode
display.description: 1 if the cluster is a designated replica cluster (replicating from an external primary), 0 for a standalone primary cluster.

display.name: Streaming Replicas
display.description: Number of streaming replicas connected to the primary.

display.name: Switchover Required
display.description: 1 if a manual switchover is required (the operator cannot promote automatically). Should normally be 0.

display.name: Cluster Up
display.description: 1 if all selected PostgreSQL instances are up. Uses the minimum across pods, so any down instance drops it to 0.

display.name: PostgreSQL Version
display.description: Major.minor PostgreSQL version reported by the cluster.
